titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Alvin Feinman Papers
titlestmt.author: by Annalise Hennessey

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 7, 2023 at 03:10

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains correspondence, writings, photographs, personal papers, electronic files, and other papers by or relating to Alvin Feinman. Among the correspondents are Feinman's wife, Deborah Dorfman, Harold Bloom, James Geary, Reginald Shepherd, and Danny Myerson. Writings consist of notes and drafts of Feinman's poetry, articles, essays, and other works. Included are materials pertaining to Preambles and Other Poems and the posthumous publication of Corrupted Into Song . The collection also contains correspondence, notes, and other papers of Deborah Dorfman.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 10 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Correspondence, 1949-2004. II. Writings, 1951-2005, undated. III. Personal Papers, 1943-2002, undated. IV. Computer Media, 1993, undated.

bioghist.head: Alvin Feinman (1929-2008)
bioghist.p: Alvin Feinman was born on November 21, 1929, in New York City. He attended Brooklyn College and the University of Chicago and completed graduate coursework at Yale University before briefly teaching in Yale's Department of Philosophy. Feinman then taught literature at Bennington College, Vermont, from 1969 to 1994. In addition to teaching, Feinman was a poet whose works include Preambles and Other Poems (1964). After Feinman's death in 2008, literary critic Harold Bloom published Corrupted into Song: The Compete Poems of Alvin Feinman . It contains fifty-seven previously published pieces and thirty-nine unpublished works found within Feinman's collection of manuscripts.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Alvin Feinman Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, writings, photographs, personal papers, electronic files, and other papers by or relating to Alvin Feinman. Among the correspondents are Feinman's wife, Deborah Dorfman, Harold Bloom, James Geary, Reginald Shepherd, and Danny Myerson. Writings consist of notes and drafts of Feinman's poetry, articles, essays, and other works. Included are materials pertaining to Preambles and Other Poems and the posthumous publication of Corrupted Into Song . The collection also contains correspondence, notes, and other papers of Deborah Dorfman.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Printed material received with the collection was removed for separate cataloging and can be accessed by searching the library's online catalog.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Alvin Feinman Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of correspondence of Alvin Feinman with his friends and colleagues, including Harold Bloom, James Geary, Danny Myerson, and Reginald Shepherd, and correspondence with immediate and extended family, including Feinman's wife, Deborah Dorfman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Associated material: Correspondence can also be found in Series III. Personal Papers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent or file title, with unidentified correspondence filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: Wright, Jay [includes draft of letter from Feinman]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series primarily consists of drafts, notes, reviews, and other materials related to poetry, articles, essays, and various projects by Alvin Feinman. The series also contains works by other writers including Harold Bloom, David Bromwich, and Michael Gottlieb.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Collected Poems, Individual Poems, Shorter Works, and Loose Notes and Notebooks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes drafts of published and unpublished works created by Feinman, including Preambles and Other Poems and Corrupted into Song . Some files contain correspondence.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title or file title.

did.unittitle: Cards and poems for James - Corrupted Into Song
DD editing notes and transcriptions for Corrupted Into Song
Poems published in Corrupted with annotations
Poems published in Corrupted with corrections
Poems published in Corrupted from Hold (Poss)
Poems published in Corrupted from Leave Alone
Poems published in Corrupted from Preambles
Poems published in Corrupted [from envelope in Feinman's drafts]
Poems published in Corrupted from "Wine" folder
Prose - not for inclusion but useful to James Geary - Corrupted Into Song
Drafts - Apostrophes to the Mortal Blow
Preambles , galleys, letter from John Weiner
Typed MS of Preambles with rejected poems

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries features drafts, notes, and materials related to individual poems by Feinman.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title or file title.

did.unittitle: Current transcriptions for first batch poems
Most recent poems - not yet dealt with
University of California Santa Barbara letter paper
Yale and Brooklyn College letter paper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes book and poetry reviews, essays, and other shorter works by Feinman and other writers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged under two subheadings: Works by Alvin Feinman and Writings of Others. Within each subheading, materials are arranged alphabetically by title or file title.

did.unittitle: AF's remembrance of Tristman and more
Rorty articles - some with AF marginalia
Notes on Sartre's What Is Literature?
Offprint from National Gallery from John Cauman
Related to "Bennington Purge" - James Lasdun and Helen Elam

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains folders of autograph manuscript notes by Alvin Feinman and Deborah Dorfman on varied topics, as well, as journals, notebooks, and planners.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged under two subheadings: Loose Notes and Notebooks. Within each subheading, materials are arranged alphabetically by file title.

did.unittitle: Various teaching notes - poetry as symmetry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes materials related to Alvin Feinman's early life and education, financial and legal records, photographs, and artwork, as well as papers of Feinman's wife, Deborah Dorfman, which contain a small amount of correspondence, notebooks, photographs of Feinman, and other materials.

did.unittitle: DD and JG communication re Corrupted
DD correspondence from McKee with ref. to JG

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. consult Access Services for further information.
